studyName: A Study to Assess the Cardiovascular, Cognitive, and Subjective Effects of Atomoxetine in Combination with Intravenous Methamphetamine

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains study number, subject IDs, sex, age, ethnicity, family race, affection status of the subject for methamphetamine abuse, how many days the participant drank beer, wine, and hard liquor, smoked marijuana, or used methamphetamine in the past 30 days, age of when the participant first used methamphetamine, education of the participant, highest schooling the participant completed, how many cigarettes the participants smoke per day, how many grams of methamphetamine the participant had in the last week, and how many years the participant was heavily using methamphetamine.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of subject for methamphetamine abuse

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: The current age when participated in study

variables.name: AGEOFONSET
variables.description: Age of when participant first used methamphetamine

variables.name: BEERP30
variables.description: How many days in the past 30 days the participant drank beer

variables.name: CIGSPERDAY
variables.description: How many cigarettes the participants smokes per day

variables.name: EDUCATION
variables.description: How many years of school the participant had completed

variables.name: EDUCATION_OTHER
variables.description: Highest schooling the participant completed

variables.name: ETHNICITY
variables.description: The fact or state of belonging to a social group of common national or cultural tradition

variables.name: FAMILY_RACE
variables.description: Family race

variables.name: GRAMSLWK
variables.description: How many grams of methamphetamine the participant had in the last week

variables.name: HVYUSE
variables.description: How many years the participant was heavily using methamphetamine

variables.name: LIQOURP30
variables.description: How many days in the past 30 days the participant drank hard liquor

variables.name: MAP30
variables.description: How many days in the past 30 days the participant used methamphetamine

variables.name: MJP30
variables.description: How many days in the past 30 days the participant smoked marijuana

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: The state of being male or female

variables.name: STUDY
variables.description: NIDA study number

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: WINEP30
variables.description: How many days in the past 30 days the participant drank wine

tables.description: The subject pedigree table contains family ID, subject ID, mother ID, father ID, sex, and twin ID.

variables.name: FAMILY_ID
variables.description: Family ID

variables.name: FATHER
variables.description: Father Subject ID

variables.name: MOTHER
variables.description: Mother Subject ID

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Unique Subject ID

variables.name: TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins or multiples

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, subject aliases, and affection status of the subject for methamphetamine abuse.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for methamphetamine abuse

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample aliases and sample use.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where samples originate

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SOURCE_SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site, and analyte type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID
